description: Galician drug trafficking clan founded in 1975 by Manuel Charlín Gama and his family. Based in Galicia, Spain, they engaged in smuggling of food, tobacco, hashish, and later cocaine and heroin. The clan was dismantled in Operation Nécora in 1990 but continued operations, with members serving prison sentences. They are considered one of the most important drug trafficking clans in Galicia.

country_links.evidence_quote: introducing scrap metal and penicillin into Spain from Portugal
country_links.context: The clan sourced scrap metal and penicillin from Portugal.
